summary: Focused verification of two bug fixes: (1) useScanner.js bad import path './api' → '../api'; (2) new ESLint 9 flat config eslint.config.js. Both PASS. Frontend now compiles cleanly (latest webpack log line: 'Compiled successfully!'), no 'Module not found' errors. All 5 target pages (/, /scan, /events, /events/obsidian-chapter-i, /my-tickets) return HTTP 200, have no webpack error overlay in HTML, and produce zero JS page-errors (uncaught exceptions). The /scan auth wall renders 'DOOR SCANNER' + 'SIGN IN' correctly, proving useOfflineScanQueue + useQrCamera hooks resolve. `yarn eslint src/hooks/useScanner.js src/pages/Scan.jsx` → 0 errors, 1 warning (unused React import in Scan.jsx — warning is allowed), exit code 0. Backend regression sanity also PASS: GET /api/events?upcoming=true → 200 with 2 events; POST /api/seed idempotent (returns {seeded:false, reason:'already has data'} on both calls).

issues: Pre-existing console warning: 'An empty string was passed to the src attribute'. Not related to this iteration's fixes — a component is rendering which triggers a browser re-download. Low priority.

critical_code_review_comments: useScanner.js line 2 correctly imports from '../api' (was './api'). The two hooks (useOfflineScanQueue, useQrCamera) are cleanly separated with clear single responsibility.

eslint.config.js flat-config is well-scoped: ignores build/, node_modules/, plugins/, coverage/, and src/components/ui/ (shadcn). Reasonable rule set (react-hooks/rules-of-hooks: error, exhaustive-deps: warn, no-unused-vars: warn with _ ignore). No changes needed.
Minor lint nit found: Scan.jsx line 1 `import React ...` is unused (react/jsx-runtime is on) and triggers the eslint warning. Cosmetic only. Can be removed but does NOT block anything.

useScanner.js uses `process.env.NODE_ENV !== 'production'` for debug logging — good defensive practice. Also correctly gates BarcodeDetector behind feature-detection and returns a user-friendly error string when unsupported.

success_rate.frontend: 100% (5/5 pages load, 0 compile errors, 0 pageerrors, eslint pass)

test_credentials: None required — auth wall visible without signing in.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Both fixes verified. If further scan-flow testing is needed, it requires an admin or door-role token (see /app/memory/test_credentials.md — mint via mongosh). The 401 console errors seen on all pages are expected: /api/me returns 401 for unauthenticated visitors; useAuth handles this and renders the anonymous view. Not a bug.
rca_of_the_issue: Original bug: useScanner.js was written with a self-referential import path 'from ./api' — which resolves relative to the file's directory (/app/frontend/src/hooks/), where no api.js exists (the actual module is at /app/frontend/src/api.js). Fix: changed to '../api' so it resolves from src/. Verified by webpack log flipping from 'Failed to compile / Module not found' → 'Compiled successfully!' and by the /scan page rendering its auth wall without any pageerror. Secondary bug: `yarn eslint` failed because CRA 5 uses eslint 8-style config embedded in package.json, but eslint v9 (installed here) requires a flat eslint.config.js at project root. Fix: added eslint.config.js with @eslint/js recommended + react/react-hooks/jsx-a11y/import plugins. Verified by running `yarn eslint src/hooks/useScanner.js src/pages/Scan.jsx` → exit 0.
